title: Obstruction Bridge PASS: class 4 (in spectrum, v_3=0) — no obstruction, reachability claim unconstrained
_fixture_purpose: Second bridge certificate. The presenting party claims target_arithmetic_class=4 is reachable (claimed_reachable=true). The bridge validator confirms: v_3(4)=0, so 4 is in the spectrum of f(b,e) mod 9 — no arithmetic obstruction. The arithmetic side of the theory does not block a control cert from claiming a target in class 4. Note: the bridge cert certifies ABSENCE of obstruction only. Actual reachability via a specific plan/control path still requires a valid [106] PASS cert. The bridge is a necessary-but-not-sufficient filter. Cert result: PASS.

bridge_claim.statement: Arithmetic class 4 is in the spectrum of f(b,e) mod 9 (v_3(4)=0). No arithmetic obstruction from [111] blocks a control cert from claiming a target in this class. For example, f(2,0)=4 — the state pair (b=2,e=0) is a valid QA state with norm 4. A plan/control cert from [106] that drives a system to a state with arithmetic fingerprint 4 faces no norm-form barrier.
bridge_claim.notes: This fixture certifies obstruction ABSENCE. It does not itself certify that class 4 is reachable — that requires an [106]-family PASS cert with a witnessed plan/control sequence. The bridge is a pre-filter, not a full reachability witness.

validation_checks.details: OBSTRUCTION_ABSENT with claimed_reachable=true: consistent — no arithmetic barrier
